item.title: EFF is Leaving X
item.description: After almost twenty years on the platform, EFF is logging off of X. This isn’t a decision we made lightly, but it might be overdue.

item.title: Help Keep Thunderbird Alive!
item.description: Thunderbird is a free email application that’s easy to set up and customize - and it’s loaded with great features!

item.title: Claude mixes up who said what, and that's not OK
item.description: Claude sometimes sends messages to itself and then thinks those messages come from the user. This is categorically distinct from hallucinations or missing permissions.

item.title: Open source security at Astral
item.description: Insights and guidance from our engineering team on how Astral secures its tools.

item.title: John Deere to Pay $99 Million in Monumental Right-to-Repair Settlement
item.description: The ag manufacturing giant will also make digital diagnostic, maintenance, and repair tools available to third parties for 10 years.

item.title: USB for Software Developers | WerWolv
item.description: A basic introduction to USB for people that don't need to know what happens on the wire

item.title: How Pizza Tycoon simulated traffic on a 25 MHz CPU — Pizza Legacy Blog
item.description: Reverse engineering the traffic system from Pizza Tycoon (1994): how the original devs drove dozens of cars through a city with almost no CPU budget.

item.title: Little Snitch for Linux
item.description: Discover powerful applications such as Little Snitch Mini, Little Snitch, LaunchBar and Micro Snitch.

item.title: Kalman Filter Explained Through Examples
item.description: Easy and intuitive Kalman Filter tutorial

item.title: Is Hormuz Open Yet?
item.description: Live status of the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: I’ve been waiting over a month for Anthropic support to respond to my billing issue
item.description: In early March, I noticed approximately $180 in unexpected charges to my Anthropic account. I’m a Claude Max subscriber, and between March 3-5, I received 16 separate “Extra Usage” invoices ranging from $10-$13 each, all in quick succession of one another. However, I wasn’t using Claude. I was away from my laptop entirely and was out sailing with my parents back home in San Diego.

item.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
item.description: Today, we’re excited to introduce Muse Spark, the first in the Muse family of models developed by Meta Superintelligence Labs. Muse Spark is a natively multimodal reasoning model with support for tool-use, visual chain of thought, and multi-agent orchestration.

item.title: The journey to a datacenter in a box
item.description: It all started with silence. The hum of my old, decapitalized laptop, the one running my entire homelab and personal services, was gone. It had died. And the timing couldn’t have been worse. My personal services were down, my internal tools were offline, and I simply didn’t have the bandwidth to drop everything and spend a weekend performing a forensic recovery.

item.title: The Building Block Economy
item.description: The most effective way to build software and get massive adoption is no longer high quality mainline apps but via building blocks that enable and encourage others to build quantity over quality.1

item.title: Do not forget how you got here
item.description: This is a note to myself Don’t forget how you got here. During the whole of school you were essentially self taught. You did not pay any attention in class, yet you still managed to get through 13 years of school. You organised a rally trip from Madrid to Ulan Bator. You did this in a seat 127 that fell to pieces. No one believed it was possible, specially since the car would not even start 3 days before the trip was to start.

item.title: The sad state of smart homes
item.description: "A camel is a horse designed by committee." - Sir Alec Issigonis I am enamored by the idea of the smart home. Devices working to make life a little easier. Over the last few years, I've been adding smart home devices to our home, mainly for simple purposes like turning the porch lights or Chri

item.title: LambdaLemon
item.description: Now with 100% more gay!

item.title: Why Claude Mythos is not AGI
item.description: Claude Mythos Preview is not AGI.

item.title: Why I still build apps for myself
item.description: Why I still build small iOS apps for myself, even in an App Store where discovery is weak and indie development often feels irrational.

item.title: Verifying human authorship with human.json
item.description: Vouching for other people's websites with this brand new protocol that I thought would be fun to implement!

item.title: Full Text Search with IndexedDB
item.description: While working on Borogove there has been a desire to have full-text search of locally-stored chat message history. For web-based apps the main storage engine in use is IndexedDB, which is a rather low-level system and certainly doesn’t have easy facilities for this kind of operation. So what’s the simplest, performant way to implement a full-text search on IndexedDB?

item.title: I Tested Meta Muse Spark Against 4 Frontier Models
item.description: Three tests, five models, zero retries. Meta crushed vision and analysis but face-planted on code. ChatGPT wrote the worst code and produced the best output. Meta wins overall — barely.

item.title: Mindfulness, Programming, and the Mistake I Made
item.description: I tried to bring mindfulness into programming and thought I was failing. Measuring my attention revealed the real issue — I was using the wrong model for what mindfulness should feel like while coding.

item.title: Self-host your homelab tunnels on bunny.net
item.description: Principal software engineer with deep experience in full-stack TypeScript, custom web browsers and real-time, media-focused systems.

item.title: Claude Is Dead
item.description: In 1882 Nietzsche claimed that the dominant paradigm in Western society at the time, the existence of the Christian God and the guidelines for morality stemming from Christian tradition can no longer be justified. And went to shock the world (or at least the 7 people that used to read him at the time) and claimed that God is dead. So let me paraphrase him and try to be as flippant and outrageous as I can, and claim that the first modern god of the AI era is dead.
